programmes.show_description: Anne Luisa Kramb joins the Lithuanian State Symphony Orchestra under Luis Toro Araya in Mendelssohn's Violin Concerto, plus Brahms's Tragic Overture and Dvorák's 7th Symphony.
Tom McKinney presents Radio 3's Breakfast show. To get in touch, email 3breakfast@bbc.co.uk.
Georgia Mann plays the best in classical music, with discoveries and surprises rubbing shoulders with familiar favourites.
Mark Forrest introduces specially made recordings of orchestral music from Prague and chamber music from Vaughan-WIlliams and his contemporaries from LSO St Luke's in London.

programmes.show_name: Composer of the Week: Thomas Adès
programmes.show_description: 2/5. Donald Macleod talks to Thomas Adès about his second opera, based on Shakespeare's The Tempest, and the unique creative process of writing for the stage.
Alessio Bax, Paul Watkins, Radovan Vlatkovic and Robert Hollingworth.
A mixtape bursting with classical favourites and lesser-known gems, with music by composers from Chopin and Beethoven to Caroline Shaw and Samuel Coleridge-Taylor.
Patricia Kopatchinskaja plays Berg's Violin Concerto with the London Symphony Orchestra under conductor Gianandrea Noseda, with Rachmaninoff's 1st Symphony also on the programme.
...Cities of the Imagination. Erland journeys deep into the heart of the Sahara's Great Sand Sea to uncover the mystery of Zerzura.

Piano trios by Chaminade, Ravel and Shostakovich with the Sitkovetsky Trio. Jonathan Swain presents.
Tom McKinney presents Radio 3's Breakfast show. To get in touch, email 3breakfast@bbc.co.uk.

The BBC Philharmonic Orchestra present a concert to welcome the arrival of Spring inspired by the seasons, plus music by Vaughan Williams and his contemporaries from LSO St. Luke's.
From St Barnabas Church, Ealing, London, with Imperial College Chamber Choir.

programmes.show_name: Composer of the Week: Thomas Adès
programmes.show_description: 3/5. Donald Macleod asks Thomas Adès about his forays into performing and taking up roles as music director for Birmingham Contemporary Music Group and the Aldeburgh Festival.
Live music from the Sarah L King trio and award-winning British pianist Thomas Kelly.
Half an hour of musical delights, including Purcell, MacMillan, piano music by Hélène de Montgeroult and John Williams' haunting theme from Schindler's List.
From Manchester's Bridgewater Hall, the BBC Philharmonic, conducted by Ludovic Morlot, plays love-inspired music by Prokofiev, Cassandra Miller and Berg.
